name: Lead Masks Case
description: The Lead Masks Case, also known as the Vintém Hill Mystery, is an unsolved incident that occurred in Niterói, Brazil, in August 1966. Two electronics technicians, Manoel Pereira da Cruz and Miguel José Viana, were found dead on Vintém Hill, each wearing lead masks. Proponents suggest the masks were intended to shield them from radiation during a UFO encounter or a secret scientific experiment gone wrong. The men also possessed a notebook with cryptic instructions and references to 'waiting for a signal' and 'negative vibrations'. Some theories propose involvement of a secret society or clandestine research. Mainstream explanations focus on more mundane possibilities, such as suicide, accidental poisoning, or murder. The lack of definitive evidence and conflicting reports have fueled speculation and conspiracy theories for decades. The autopsy results were inconclusive, and the exact cause of death remains unknown. Critics point out the possibility of misinterpretation of the notebook entries and the lack of verifiable evidence supporting extraterrestrial involvement. The case highlights the human tendency to seek extraordinary explanations when confronted with the unexplained.
subtitle: Unsolved mystery on Vintém Hill.

claims.claim: The men were waiting for contact with extraterrestrials.
claims.evidence: The lead masks were protection against radiation from a UFO, and the notebook mentioned waiting for a signal.
claims.criticism: No UFOs were sighted, and lead masks are not effective protection against most forms of radiation. The notebook entries are open to interpretation.

claims.claim: The men were involved in a secret scientific experiment that went wrong.
claims.evidence: The lead masks, notebook entries, and the men's profession suggest experimentation.
claims.criticism: No evidence of any experiment has been found, and the materials found on the scene are insufficient to determine the nature of any potential experiment.

claims.claim: The men were murdered as part of a conspiracy.
claims.evidence: The unusual circumstances of their deaths and the lack of a clear motive point to foul play.
claims.criticism: There is no evidence to suggest murder, and the possibility of suicide or accidental poisoning cannot be ruled out. The masks could have been part of a botched attempt at self-treatment.

keyFigures.name: Manoel Pereira da Cruz
keyFigures.role: One of the deceased electronics technicians.

keyFigures.name: Miguel José Viana
keyFigures.role: One of the deceased electronics technicians.

timeline.event: Manoel Pereira da Cruz and Miguel José Viana are found dead on Vintém Hill.
Initial investigation and media coverage intensify speculation.
The case remains unsolved, continuing to fuel speculation and debate.
